assertions.title: Round 17391 malformed provisional-score hardening survives apply, reports skips, and stays rerun-stable

steps.action: Capture pre-apply API snapshots for challenge, phases, resources, submissions, and review summations on challenge b983de6f-cc7f-463e-867c-87e54f3b72f1.
steps.expected: Baseline identity sets are readable before rerun comparison.
steps.observed: Challenge lookup returned exactly one challenge with stable three-phase roster; API snapshots showed 112 submitter resources, 248 submissions, and 168 review summations (16 final, 152 provisional) with zero duplicate identity signals.

steps.action: Run standard apply for round 17391 with Node 18.19.0 and an isolated skipped-file path.
steps.expected: Apply completes successfully instead of aborting on malformed provisional-score data.
steps.observed: First apply exited 0 with APPLY_RECORD status=existing and APPLY_SUMMARY errors=0.

steps.action: Inspect APPLY_RECORD / APPLY_SUMMARY and the first skipped artifact for malformed provisional-score handling.
steps.expected: The malformed row for submission -403380560001 / coder 22836077 is explicitly skipped with the importer's exact reason code rather than crashing the run.
steps.observed: The skipped artifact recorded round 17391 / member 22836077 / legacySubmissionId -403380560001 with reasonCode=malformed-provisional-score; first-run skipped artifact reason codes were ['finalist-without-attachable-submission', 'malformed-provisional-score', 'missing-member'] with 645 total records.

steps.action: Verify that well-formed provisional-score rows still reconcile during the same apply.
steps.expected: Well-formed provisional rows are still imported or reconciled normally while malformed rows are reported separately.
steps.observed: First apply provisional reconciliation balanced exactly to 437 legacy non-example provisional rows: imported/reconciled=152 (alreadyPresent=152, created=0), malformedSkipped=96, missingMemberSkipped=189.

steps.action: Capture post-first snapshots, rerun standard apply with a second isolated skipped-file path, and compare post-first vs post-second identity sets.
steps.expected: Second apply exits successfully and challenge/phase/resource/submission/review identity sets stay stable without duplicates.
steps.observed: Second apply exited 0 with the same reconciliation totals, the two skipped artifacts were byte-for-byte equivalent as JSON content, and post-first vs post-second challenge ids / phase ids / resource tuples / submission legacy ids / review ids / final review keys / provisional review keys were all stable (sha256s identical in verification-summary.json).

frictions.description: Round 17391 was already partially imported in the shared environment before this smoke test, so the standard apply validation exercised the existing/reconciliation path rather than a pristine create path.
frictions.resolution: Captured pre/post API snapshots and relied on APPLY_RECORD reconciliation counters plus rerun identity-set diffs to validate the hardening on the live shared fixture.

summary: MISC-HARDENING-SMOKE-001 passed. Both standard apply runs for round 17391 exited 0, the malformed provisional row for submission -403380560001 / coder 22836077 was explicitly reported with reasonCode `malformed-provisional-score`, well-formed provisional rows still reconciled successfully in the same run, and the second rerun preserved stable challenge/phase/resource/submission/review identity sets; validation-state.json should not be updated from this synthetic smoke flow.
